published.text:
### Existing page context
Heading: AI Lead Follow-Up for Service Businesses
Hero: Most service businesses lose leads because they respond too slowly. AI follow-up fixes that by contacting every lead within seconds and staying on them until they book or opt out.
Sub: Below: how it works, what it costs, which businesses benefit most, and how to tell if it is worth the investment for yours.

### New section
Heading: What a first lead-follow-up build usually includes
Body: Most service businesses start with a focused system that covers the highest-impact gap — not a full omnichannel overhaul. A realistic first build typically includes these pieces: instant first-touch via SMS and email; a 5-to-7-touch follow-up sequence over 7 to 14 days; missed-call recovery; CRM tagging and status updates; and qualified-lead handoff. The section explicitly routes to the instant lead response page, missed-call text-back page, cost guide, estimate follow-up page, AI appointment setter page, maintenance/support page, and the no-tech-team guide.
Sub: This scope usually takes 2 to 4 weeks to build and costs between $2,000 and $5,000 depending on channels, CRM complexity, and qualification logic.

### FAQ
Question: What does a realistic first lead-follow-up build include?
Answer: A typical first build covers instant SMS and email response to new inquiries, a 5-to-7-touch follow-up sequence over 7 to 14 days, missed-call text-back recovery, CRM tagging so your team sees lead status without manual updates, and a qualified-lead handoff that routes ready leads to a team member or directly onto a calendar. This scope usually takes 2 to 4 weeks and costs between $2,000 and $5,000 depending on channels, CRM complexity, and qualification logic.

### Final CTA
Heading: Book a 30-Minute Call
Body: Service-business owners who need faster response, clearer scope, and a realistic first-phase quote can use this page to decide whether to start with lead follow-up, missed-call recovery, estimate follow-up, or a broader booking workflow.

edits.description: Shipped as a bounded indexing-support refresh for an existing parent workflow page instead of creating a net-new URL.
The new section is intentionally answer-extractable: it defines what a first production-worthy lead-follow-up build includes in plain buyer language, with no hype and no fake proof.
The copy pushes readers and crawlers into real adjacent workflow pages so the parent guide acts more clearly like a cluster hub instead of a generic overview.
The FAQ mirrors the new section so the page’s existing FAQPage schema can expose the same scope answer more directly to search and AI systems.
Tone stays practical and specific: realistic first build, realistic timeline, realistic cost band, and explicit routing to narrower pages when the leak is really missed calls, estimates, or booking.
